COALINGA REGIONAL MEDICAL CTR DP/SNF

Overview

Address
1191 PHELPS AVE., COALINGA, CA 93210

Avg daily residents

Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 23.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-04-10 8 3 5 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-26 13 9 4 88 1 88
Total weighted health score
73.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (39)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

35 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
4 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-11-12 2025-12-03
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-11-12 2025-12-03
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-07-17 2025-08-31
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-07-17 2025-08-31
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2025-04-10 2025-04-12
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. F 2025-04-10 2025-04-12
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2025-04-10 2025-04-12
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-15
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-10-17 2024-11-08
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-06-07 2024-06-08
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-25
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-01-26 2024-02-25
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2024-01-26 2024-02-25
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2024-01-26 2024-02-25
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-25
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-01-26 2024-02-25
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2024-01-26 2024-02-25
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-25
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-11-09 2023-11-23
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-11-08 2023-11-22
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. H 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. G 2021-10-13 2021-12-30
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2021-10-13 2021-12-30
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. H 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2021-10-13 2021-12-30
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. D 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2021-10-13 2021-12-30
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2021-10-13 2021-12-30
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0836 Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. D 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0840 Employ or obtain outside professional resources to provide services in the nursing home when the facility does not employ a qualified professional to furnish a required service. H 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. F 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2021-10-13 2022-06-01
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. E 2021-10-13 2021-12-30
